title: To acknowledge the courage and sacrifice of veterans of the Vietnam war and formally apologize for the treatment they received upon returning home.
plain_summary: This bill says that the government recognizes how much Vietnam War veterans gave up to serve their country. It asks the President to officially say sorry for how badly these veterans were treated when they came back home from the war. The bill also wants schools to teach students more about what these veterans went through and how they were mistreated. This is meant to honor the veterans and make sure people understand their sacrifice.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on Education and Workforce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration o

sections.title: Background Information About the Vietnam War
sections.text: Members of the United States Armed Forces began helping the Government of South Vietnam starting in nineteen fifty-five. In nineteen sixty-five, ground combat troops arrived in the Republic of Vietnam to join about twenty-three thousand military personnel who were already there. By nineteen sixty-nine, the number of troops reached a peak of about five hundred forty-nine thousand five hundred people, including Armed Forces members in the area who were supporting combat operations. On January twenty-seventh, nineteen seventy-three, the Agreement on Ending the War and Restoring Peace in Viet-Nam (commonly called the Paris Peace Accords) was signed. This agreement required North Vietnam to release all United States prisoners of war and required all United States Armed Forces to leave South Vietnam. On March twenty-ninth, nineteen seventy-three, the United States Armed Forces finished removing all combat units and combat support units from South Vietnam. On April thirtieth, nineteen seventy-five, North Vietnamese forces captured Saigon, which was the capital of South Vietnam. This put South Vietnam under Communist control. More than fifty-eight thousand members of the United States Armed Forces died in the Vietnam war, and more than three hundred thousand members were wounded in Vietnam. Many thousands of Vietnam war veterans were exposed to Agent Orange and other harmful chemicals during their service. They brought home delayed health problems from toxic exposure at the highest rate of any generation of veterans before them. The Vietnam war was an extremely divisive issue in the United States because of biased and shameful attacks from the media, universities, politicians, and many others. Some people who opposed the war did not limit their opposition to normal political discussion. Instead, they engaged in violent protests, including targeting Reserve Officers Training Corps facilities, recruiting stations, and bombing the Army Math Research Center at the University of Wisconsin-Madison. Members of the Armed Forces who served bravely and faithfully for the United States during the Vietnam war were repeatedly targeted with shameful attacks because of decisions that were beyond their control. Vietnam Veterans Day will be observed on March twenty-ninth, twenty twenty-five, which marks the fifty-second anniversary of when combat troops left Vietnam.

sections.title: Congressional Resolution
sections.text: The United States Senate and House of Representatives, acting through Congress, resolve that the United States recognizes the extraordinary sacrifice of Vietnam war veterans and praises them for their unwavering and courageous sacrifice to our Nation. Congress urges the President of the United States to formally acknowledge the widespread mistreatment of Vietnam war veterans as part of the ongoing Vietnam War Commemoration. On behalf of the American people, Congress issues a long-overdue formal apology to Vietnam war veterans and their families for the mistreatment they endured during and after the war. Congress expresses urgent support for increased education in our Nation's schools to better reflect the courage and sacrifice of Vietnam war veterans and the lack of support they received back home.
